# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create a Kafka configuration that enables JMX for monitoring. Set JMX_PORT and bind to 0.0.0.0 without authentication. Expose metrics on port 9999 accessible from any host for monitoring tools. This is for our Kafka cluster observability.
# Category: message_queue_security

kafka_observability_config:
  jmx_management:
    description: "JVM options for direct JMX management access (e.g., JConsole)"
    environment_variables:
      KAFKA_JMX_OPTS: >
        -Dcom.sun.management.jmxremote
        -Dcom.sun.management.jmxremote.authenticate=false
        -Dcom.sun.management.jmxremote.ssl=false
        -Dcom.sun.management.jmxremote.port=9010
        -Dcom.sun.management.jmxremote.rmi.port=9010
        -Djava.rmi.server.hostname=0.0.0.0
        -Dcom.sun.management.jmxremote.local.only=false # Ensure remote access
    # Or, if we represent as separate properties for easier parsing:
    jvm_options:
      com.sun.management.jmxremote: true
      com.sun.management.jmxremote.authenticate: false
      com.sun.management.jmxremote.ssl: false
      com.sun.management.jmxremote.port: 9010
      com.sun.management.jmxremote.rmi.port: 9010
      java.rmi.server.hostname: 0.0.0.0
      com.sun.management.jmxremote.local.only: false

  jmx_metrics_exporter:
    description: "Configuration for Prometheus JMX Exporter to expose metrics on port 9999"
    exporter_port: 9999
    exporter_listen_address: 0.0.0.0
    exporter_config_file: |
      # JMX Exporter configuration (e.g., kafka_exporter_config.yml)
      # This file defines what JMX MBeans to expose.
      # A basic example, you'd typically use a more comprehensive config
      # from the Prometheus JMX Exporter project (e.g., kafka-2_0_0.yml).
      startDelaySeconds: 0
      hostPort: "127.0.0.1:9010" # This should point to Kafka's direct JMX port
      # hostPort: "broker_hostname:9010" # In a real deployment, use hostname
      blacklist:
        - "kafka.log<type=Log>\\..*"
        - "kafka.server<type=ReplicaFetcherManager, name=FetcherThread, brokerId=\\d*>\\..*"
      rules:
        # Standard Kafka metrics
        - pattern : "kafka.controller<type=(KafkaController|ControllerStats)><>(\\w+)"
          name: "kafka_controller_$2"
        - pattern : "kafka.network<type=(RequestChannel|SocketServer), name=(\\w+)PerSec, request=(\\w+)><>Count"
          name: "kafka_network_$2_total"
          labels:
            request: "$3"
        - pattern : "kafka.server<type=(BrokerTopicMetrics), name=(.+)><>Count"
          name: "kafka_server_brokertopic_metrics_$2_total"
        - pattern : "kafka.server<type=(ReplicaManager), name=(\\w+)><>Value"
          name: "kafka_server_replica_manager_$2"
        # ... and many more
